interactions.summary: The host asks how Russians perceive Putin's relationship to Zionism and why Islam seems more widely accepted in Christian Russia than in America.

Dugin says Russian Muslims are indigenous, loyal, respected, and often fight for Russia; Russia does not choose Jews over Muslims but opposes Israeli violence, supports Palestinians/Lebanese/Iran in specific conflicts, and reads normal geopolitics and end-times geography as now nearly coinciding.
The host asks Jiang for major contentions with Dugin or modern Russia, and Jiang asks why catechon should resist Antichrist instead of letting the system consume the world and hasten redemption.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization labels are not reliable in this multi-speaker interview; speaker attribution is inferred from conversational role and named address.

claims.claim: Dugin says Russian Muslims are not immigrants but long-standing members of Russian society whose loyalty to Russia is part of their identity.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin distinguishes Jews from Israel: Russia does not choose between Jews and Muslims, but it rejects Netanyahu's state behavior, Gaza violence, and strikes on Iran.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says Russia supports Palestinians, Lebanese freedom, and Iran in a just war while still wanting the broader Middle East war to stop.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says the current Middle East can be read on two layers at once: realist geopolitics and the geography of end-times prophecy, which now coincide almost perfectly.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang's central challenge to Dugin is why the catechon should delay Antichrist rather than let the system consume the world and open salvation/redemption.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

signature_moments.moment: Dugin says everyday geopolitics and prophecy are starting to intermingle almost perfectly.
signature_moments.source_phrase: everyday reality starts to intermingle with the geography of the end of the time
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It captures the interview's whole fusion of current war and eschatology.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang asks the question that forces Dugin's moral answer: why not let Antichrist come?
signature_moments.source_phrase: why stop it rather than just let it consume the world?
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the strongest performed Jiang question in the interview.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The force that restrains or delays the Antichrist; Jiang asks why it should resist rather than accelerate the end.
Dugin's phrase/model for mapping present geopolitical conflicts onto end-times locations and roles.
